data.summary: The USDA Forest Service Pacific Southwest Region (Region 5) issued an RFP for modernizing the Forest Inventory and Analysis (FIA) Data Platform, migrating from Oracle Forms (2012 vintage) to a cloud-native architecture on AWS GovCloud with PostgreSQL. The scope includes web application development, native iOS and Android mobile apps for field data collection, GIS integration with ArcGIS Enterprise, and data migration of approximately 2.3TB. However, the proposal submission deadline was January 15, 2025 -- this deadline has passed, making this opportunity expired and unbiddable.
data.periodOfPerformance: Base period 18 months plus two 12-month option periods for maintenance and support
data.agency: USDA Forest Service, Pacific Southwest Region (Region 5)
data.title: Forest Inventory and Analysis (FIA) Data Platform Modernization

deadlines.label: Proposal Submission Deadline (EXPIRED)
deadlines.notes: DEADLINE HAS PASSED. This deadline was January 15, 2025, which is well in the past. Late proposals will not be considered. This opportunity is no longer open for submissions.
deadlines.rawText: All proposals must be received no later than January 15, 2025, 4:00 PM Pacific Time. Late proposals will not be considered.

deadlines.label: Questions Deadline (EXPIRED)
deadlines.notes: Originally December 15, 2024. Extended to January 3, 2025 per Amendment 0002. Both dates have passed.
deadlines.rawText: All questions regarding this solicitation must be submitted in writing to R5-Acquisitions@usda.gov no later than December 15, 2024. (Extended to January 3, 2025 per Amendment 0002)

warnings: CRITICAL: The submission deadline of January 15, 2025 has passed. This opportunity is expired and no longer accepting proposals.
Estimated award date was March 2025 -- award may have already been made.

evaluationFactors: Factor 1: Technical Approach and Understanding (Most Important)
Factor 2: Relevant Experience and Past Performance (Important)
Technical Approach is significantly more important than Price. Relevant Experience and Key Personnel are approximately equal and together are more important than Price.
data.overview: Modernize the FIA data platform from Oracle Forms (2012) to a cloud-native architecture. Scope includes migrating 2.3TB Oracle database to PostgreSQL on AWS GovCloud, developing a responsive web application, building native iOS and Android mobile apps with offline capability for field data collection, implementing GIS integration with ArcGIS Enterprise 11.x, providing data migration services, conducting UAT with FIA field crews, and providing 12 months of post-deployment support. NOTE: This solicitation is expired -- the deadline was January 15, 2025.

requirements.requirement: Migrate existing FIA database (approximately 2.3TB) from Oracle to PostgreSQL on AWS GovCloud
Develop a responsive web application for data entry, query, and visualization
Develop native iOS and Android mobile applications for field data collection with offline capability
Implement GIS integration with ArcGIS Enterprise 11.x for spatial analysis
Provide data migration services including validation and reconciliation
Conduct user acceptance testing with FIA field crews
Provide 12 months of post-deployment support and maintenance

requiredVolumes: Volume I: Technical Proposal (50 page limit)
Volume II: Past Performance (10 page limit)
Volume III: Price Proposal (no page limit)

deliverables: Migrated PostgreSQL database on AWS GovCloud
Native iOS and Android mobile applications
Data migration with validation and reconciliation

redFlags.whyItMatters: The proposal submission deadline was January 15, 2025, which has already passed. The solicitation explicitly states that late proposals will not be considered. This opportunity is expired and unbiddable.

risks.flag: Contract likely already awarded (estimated award March 2025)

incumbentInfo.notes: No incumbent contractor identified. The current FIA data platform was developed in-house in 2012 using Oracle Forms.

recommendations: This opportunity is expired -- no past performance evaluation needed. For future similar opportunities, prepare references for Oracle-to-PostgreSQL migrations, mobile field data collection apps, and GIS integration projects.

data.confidenceExplanation: Very high confidence in NO_GO decision. The submission deadline of January 15, 2025 has unambiguously passed. The solicitation states 'Late proposals will not be considered.' The estimated award date was March 2025, meaning the contract has likely already been awarded. There is no basis for pursuing this opportunity.
requiredActions: DO NOT BID: The submission deadline (January 15, 2025) has passed. This opportunity is expired.
Check SAM.gov for award notice on USDA-FS-R5-2025-0089 for competitive intelligence

Track any re-solicitation of this requirement if the initial award was unsuccessful

data.summaryJustification: NO_GO. The submission deadline of January 15, 2025 has passed. This opportunity is expired and no proposal can be submitted. The estimated award date was March 2025, meaning the contract has likely already been awarded. Small Business Set-Aside requirement is noted for future reference.
blockers: EXPIRED: Submission deadline January 15, 2025 has passed. This is an absolute blocker.
data.decisionRationale: The submission deadline has unambiguously passed. Late proposals will not be considered. This is a definitive NO_GO regardless of any other factors.

data.basisOfEstimate: PRICING NOT DEVELOPED -- NO-BID. The submission deadline (January 15, 2025) has passed and this opportunity is expired. The Government estimate was approximately $1.8M for a Time-and-Materials contract covering 18-month base period plus two 12-month option periods. This was a Small Business Set-Aside.
